sourceMovie.title: Backdraft
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Backdraft works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Negotiator
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Backdraft clicked for you, The Negotiator (1998) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and thriller, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Scarface
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Backdraft clicked for you, Scarface (1983) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and action, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Backdraft?
faq.answer: Try The Negotiator, We Die Young, Scarface—each shares DNA with Backdraft while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Backdraft?
faq.answer: Backdraft (1991) is best described as Drama, Thriller, Action, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Backdraft part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Backdraft Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
